config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society matters a lot, but individual rights should not be treated as secondary by default, because once the state or the majority gets too much power, it can easily abuse people in the name of the common good. From my perspective, a healthy society is built by protecting basic rights first, then balancing them with public needs in specific cases. If we reverse that priority, it becomes too easy to justify injustice against minorities or unpopular individuals.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan di atas hak individu, karena di Amerika saya melihat terlalu banyak contoh bagaimana “demi kebaikan umum” bisa dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Hak individu bukan penghalang bagi masyarakat; justru itu pagar yang mencegah mayoritas menginjak minoritas atau orang yang tidak populer. Tentu ada keadaan tertentu, seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan, ketika pembatasan terbatas bisa dibenarkan, tetapi itu harus pengecualian yang sangat ketat, bukan prinsip utama. Kalau prioritasnya dibalik, saya pikir kita terlalu mudah kehilangan kebebasan yang justru membuat masyarakat layak disebut adil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically take priority over individual rights. From my perspective in Indonesia, I have seen how appeals to “the common good” can be used to pressure dissent, silence minorities, or excuse unfair treatment, especially when power is concentrated. Society does matter, but it is strongest when basic rights are protected first and any limits are narrow, necessary, and accountable. If we put society above the individual by default, we make it too easy for the majority or the state to decide who gets sacrificed.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu: di Amerika, saya melihat prioritas pada “kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan kebebasan yang sebenarnya tidak perlu. Poin tentang kesehatan publik dan keamanan memang valid, tetapi justru karena itu saya melihat batasan harus benar-benar sempit, terukur, dan diawasi ketat, bukan dijadikan prinsip umum yang mengalahkan hak individu. Hak individu bukan musuh masyarakat; bagi saya, hak itulah yang melindungi masyarakat dari penyalahgunaan kekuasaan oleh mayoritas atau negara. Jika kita membalik prioritasnya, terlalu mudah bagi “kebaikan bersama” menjadi alasan untuk mengorbankan orang yang paling rentan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I still disagree with the statement, because in Indonesia I have seen how “society first” can become a convenient excuse for pressure, conformity, and abuse by those in power. Public needs do matter, but they should not automatically outrank individual rights, because rights are what stop the majority from trampling minorities and inconvenient voices. I do not deny that there are limited cases like health or security where restrictions are justified, but those must be narrow, temporary, and accountable. If we make society the default priority, I think we risk turning “the common good” into a tool for injustice.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu, karena dari sudut pandang saya di Amerika, kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu. Pengalaman politik di sini menunjukkan bahwa alasan “demi kebaikan bersama” sering dipakai untuk memperluas kekuasaan negara, membungkam suara yang tidak populer, atau menekan minoritas. Memang ada situasi seperti kesehatan publik dan keamanan yang bisa membenarkan pembatasan, tetapi itu harus sempit, sementara, dan diawasi ketat, bukan dijadikan prinsip umum. Bagi saya, hak individu justru fondasi masyarakat yang adil; tanpa itu, “kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu mudah berubah menjadi pembenaran penindasan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
